label: Survey Research
definition: Primary data collected via structured online survey instrument distributed to target population

label: Benchmarking
definition: Comparative analysis using Aberdeen Competitive Framework to classify organizations as Laggard (30%), Industry Norm (50%), or Best in Class (20%)

label: Qualitative Analysis
definition: Expert judgment, interview-based findings, and qualitative interpretation of survey data and industry trends

label: Case Study
definition: Specific organizational example cited to illustrate a finding or trend (e.g., J.P. Morgan Chase/IBM)

label: Retrospective Validation
definition: Post-hoc assessment of prediction accuracy performed during archival ingest, comparing study predictions to known outcomes

label: Strategy Classification
definition: Categorization of organizational approach within the Aberdeen Competitive Framework tiers (Laggard, Industry Norm, Best in Class)

label: Viability Prediction
definition: Observation is a forward-looking prediction about future market conditions, technology adoption, or business outcomes made at time of study

label: Actual Outcome
definition: Retrospective record of what actually happened to a company, product, or technology after the study date; used to assess prescience

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Empirical evaluation of a specific technology product or system based on hands-on testing, performance measurement, or documented behavior

label: Benchmark Result
definition: Quantitative or time-based measurement from testing; a specific metric value obtained through direct measurement or structured evaluation

label: Framework Factor
definition: A component or variable within an analytical framework or model used to structure the analysis; a dimension of a multi-factor evaluation scheme

label: Market Data
definition: Quantitative or qualitative data point about market size, pricing, cost structures, or commercial dynamics derived from research

label: Expert Opinion
definition: Observation records an analyst judgment, interpretation, or recommendation that goes beyond raw data to offer evaluative conclusions

label: High Confidence
definition: Assessment or data point is well-supported by multiple sources, specific numbers, or direct document citations

label: Medium Confidence
definition: Finding supported by qualitative analysis, expert interpretation, or retrospective assessment with reasonable evidence

label: Low Confidence
definition: Finding is inferred from limited evidence in the source text or involves significant extrapolation

label: Benchmark Report
definition: Comparative study using standardized framework to evaluate organizational practices and performance across industry tiers

label: Survey Research
definition: Primary research methodology using structured questionnaires distributed to a defined population

label: Benchmarking
definition: Systematic measurement and comparison of performance using standardized test specifications with full-disclosure reporting

label: Statistical Analysis
definition: Quantitative analysis of survey data including frequency distributions, cross-tabulations, and trend analysis

label: Field Research
definition: Primary research conducted through direct interaction with products, services, or market participants in real-world settings

label: IT Budget Management
definition: Domain covering IT spending governance, cost optimization, budget processes, and alignment of IT investment with business strategy
